Pakistani TV and film comedian

**Irfan Khoosat** ([Punjabi](/wiki/Punjabi_language "Punjabi language"), [Urdu](/wiki/Urdu_language "Urdu language"): عرفان کھوسٹ) is a Pakistani actor, director, producer and comedian.[[1]](#cite_note-Dawn-1)[[2]](#cite_note-Dawn2-2)

He is famous for his comic role as *Havaldar Karamdad* in the TV series *[Andhera Ujala](/wiki/Andhera_Ujala "Andhera Ujala")* (1984–1985) in which he portrayed a simpleton and a blabbermouth character of a low-ranked policeman.[[1]](#cite_note-Dawn-1)

He is also well-known as a stage comedian.

## Early and personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Irfan_Khoosat&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early and personal life")]

Irfan Khoosat was born in [Okara](/wiki/Okara,_Pakistan "Okara, Pakistan"), [Punjab](/wiki/Punjab,_Pakistan "Punjab, Pakistan").

His father Sultan Khoosat, who died when Irfan was nine, was a painter, a former employee of [Radio Pakistan](/wiki/Radio_Pakistan "Radio Pakistan") and himself an actor and comedian.[[3]](#cite_note-:0-3) His maternal grandfather Mughal Basheer was also an actor, appearing in the 1962 Urdu-language film *[Chiragh Jalta Raha](/wiki/Chiragh_Jalta_Raha "Chiragh Jalta Raha")*.

He has been married three times including, Zahida Butt a PTV newscaster and has nine children, including notable film and TV director and actor [Sarmad Khoosat](/wiki/Sarmad_Sultan_Khoosat "Sarmad Sultan Khoosat")[[2]](#cite_note-Dawn2-2) and Kanwal Khoosat, who's also a director, [screenwriter](/wiki/Screenwriter "Screenwriter") and [actress](/wiki/Actor "Actor"). His first wife Zahida Butt died in 2002.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Early career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Irfan_Khoosat&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Early career")]

Irfan Khoosat got his first breakthrough as a child artist with [Radio Pakistan](/wiki/Radio_Pakistan "Radio Pakistan"), when he replaced an actor for the show *Ultay Pultay*.[[3]](#cite_note-:0-3)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Irfan_Khoosat&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Career")]

### Actor

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Irfan_Khoosat&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Actor")]

Irfan Khoosat began his acting career with the Punjabi-language movie *Kochwan* in 1969 and even if he's better known for his work on television he would eventually appear in more than 100 films, with 66 in Punjabi, 39 in Urdu and a single movie in Pashto, *Banke Lalya* which released in 2013.

### Director

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Irfan_Khoosat&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Director")]

Irfan Khoosat has directed the 1985 Punjabi-language film *Direct Hawaldar*, also acting in it, based on his famous role from *Andhera Ujala*.

He has also directed a television serial, *Karam Daad Aur Jaffer Hussain*, a spin-off of *Andhera Ujala*.

He has directed plays as well, including *Tokray Thallay*, which was staged at Lahore's [Alhamra Arts Council](/wiki/Alhamra_Arts_Council "Alhamra Arts Council") in 2010.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

### Producer

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Irfan_Khoosat&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Producer")]

Irfan Khoosat has produced numerous television serials, including *[Aao Kahani Buntay Hain](/wiki/Aao_Kahani_Buntay_Hain "Aao Kahani Buntay Hain")* (2011), directed by his daughter Kanwal, but concentrating mainly on comedy dramas such as *Do Aur Do Chaar* (2002) and *Shashlik Xtra Hott* (2011), both written and directed by his son Sarmad.

Telefilms that he has produced include *Tamasha Ghar* (2003), directed by Sarmad, and *Hernaam Kaur* (2003), written and directed by Sarmad, an adaptation of a [Manto](/wiki/Saadat_Hasan_Manto "Saadat Hasan Manto") short story about the [partition](/wiki/Partition_of_India "Partition of India").[[6]](#cite_note-6)
